titleStmt.title: Naameh – AD (F.67) – Maslakh Before (4)
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Lebanon Fieldwork Campaign Ana Iriarte Diez 2023

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: yes, right.
And now it's more difficult.
Right.
I wish we could go back in time, really, K, I wish.
yes right, there was war and everything but how there was...
Look, wasn't there a war and everything? It was (still) better than nowadays.
Better, yes.
People were able to live, but now people aren't able to live, how should they be able to live?
the least for an appartment, the least for an appartment, was twenty dollars, for fifty dollars, for twenty dollars, but now what?
For someone who has a family what does it mean? For someone renting? (a house)
How many people are living in rented houses and cannot (pay for it). now we they want hundred dollars for house renting.
My god.
It is increasing for us. And someone who does not have (I mean) liquidity, someone whose income is restricted.
No, life is very difficult. I turned sixty-four years old, but those days don't #. there was war.
I turned sixty-four years old, but I haven't witnessed anything like nowadays.
There was war. It is true that people were besieged Dekwaneh, in the Maslakh (area), (but) people were living.
People were living in every thing (sense).
I mean the rich and the poor wasn't feeling that there was a difference. the people were living better than those days.
You could go anywhere with one pound, or half a pound.
Now what?
Now if you want to go to Beirut, how much does it cost you?
I went down to Beirut, do you know how much we spent me and K? Six hundred thousand for the (taxi) trip.
People are now cutting down on many things.

and that who works in illegal matters, lives. That who... You and I, can we work in illegal/sinful matters? no, God is sufficient... May God suffice us with what is permissible and keep us away from what is forbidden.
Right.
Amen, oh lord.
My dear.
